finding_results.notes: The chapter now seeds the false-report danger immediately after the corridor scene, then closes by naming the hidden WISE-side lie in plain language, tying the alliance to that lie, identifying the exposure path through attendance/calendar/lateral records, and rendering the cost through Anya's physical reaction plus concrete operational fallout for both her cover and Damian's channels.

finding_results.notes: The former explanatory follow-through has been replaced with new pressure or consequence. Damian's political summary now tips directly into embodied interruption and renewed negotiation, the corridor-side beat turns into his recruitment wound and term-setting, and Anya's partial lie now produces Damian's conditional acceptance and first-channel test instead of narration re-certifying the lie.
The first paragraph now gives the elapsed time since the prior chapter and explains why Damian encounters a different public-facing name here.
The opening now starts with Brandt already leaning on Damian in public and Anya intervening under pressure, which breaks the prior chapter-opening scan-and-approach pattern.
Verified revised word count: 7879 words. Heading contract remains intact.

Replaced Damian's thesis-speech explanation with concrete, reluctant, self-correcting speech. Line 377: Damian stops mid-sentence ('My father left—'), restarts with a specific inherited procurement authorization through the Defense Liaison Office, Gesellschaft Club committee seats, and family appointment memos — grounding the political explanation in concrete inherited channels rather than abstract nouns. Line 381: second self-correction ('The point is it's not—' catches himself, starts over). Lines 383-385: Damian interrupts Anya's unfinished 'And you're—' with 'Trying to shut it down,' then immediately undercuts his own certainty ('Or slow it down. I don't know. Something.'). Removed slogans 'I'm trying to stop a business model,' 'Nationalism is the sales pitch,' 'the same five men eat first,' and the 'So yes, I'm trying to stop them' bookend. Replaced Anya's mic-drop 'We're in the same corridor. That's less ambitious' (line 427) with a visible self-correction: 'I'm not—' stops herself, then redirects to the flatter 'We're both in this hallway. That's all I'm saying.' Also de-polished Anya's response to Damian's reveal ('What do you want me to say?' replacing the punchline 'What do you want, applause?'). Both speakers now show visible interruption and self-correction; no line in the revised span functions as a stand-alone thesis or slogan.

finding_results.notes: Anya's reads now stay partial and worried: she frames Gessler as a paper-pattern threat rather than exact operational knowledge, blurts too much and has to swerve, and treats negotiation leverage as guesswork instead of precise control.
Post-revision whole-file count is 0 instances of 'just' and 1 instance of 'maybe'; the remaining 'maybe' is Damian's thought at line 431.

The opening now differentiates itself through badge-cover bureaucracy and Brandt's intrusion rather than a reused heat/noise-overload package, and the forum re-entry returns on coffee and surveillance texture instead of restarting the same room kit.

pass_summaries.summary: Rebuilt Chapter 4 around a live public-pressure opening, a fuller corridor negotiation, and an immediate official-channel consequence scene; clarified the one-week/name bridge, made Anya's false WISE assessment explicit as the hidden institutional lie behind the alliance, seeded the Bretz handoff into Chapter 5, and raised the chapter to 7879 words.

Reworked the corridor exchange so Damian's Renewal Front explanation arrives in reluctant concrete chunks (a specific inherited procurement authorization, Gesellschaft Club committee seats, family appointment memos) rather than polished slogans. Added self-correction from both speakers: Damian stops and restarts twice, cuts across Anya mid-sentence, and undercuts his own certainty; Anya aborts a defensive claim and redirects. Removed thesis-speech lines ('I'm trying to stop a business model,' 'Nationalism is the sales pitch') and the 'same corridor' aphorism. Alliance terms, chapter engine, and state deltas preserved.

Revised the chapter to remove clipped narration at the cited spans, make Anya's telepathic reads messier and less omniscient, freshen the ministry-room opening, and cut hedge-word overload below threshold while preserving the alliance formation and paper-trail consequence.
